description: Key terms and concepts used in the Sui blockchain API

terms.definition: The basic unit of storage in Sui. An object is a typed piece of data identified by a unique ObjectID. Objects can be owned, shared, or immutable.
A 32-byte unique identifier for a Sui object, represented as a hex string prefixed with 0x.
A Sui transaction block containing one or more programmable transaction commands. Atomic unit of execution on the Sui network.
A Base58-encoded hash that uniquely identifies a transaction block on Sui.
A certified commitment to the global state of the Sui network at a point in time, containing a set of transaction digests and the resulting state root.
A monotonically increasing integer identifying a checkpoint on the Sui network.
A period of time (approximately 24 hours) during which the validator set and protocol parameters remain constant. Staking rewards are distributed at epoch boundaries.
A fungible token on Sui represented as a Move object of type 0x2::coin::Coin<T>. Each coin object has a balance.
The native token of the Sui blockchain, used for gas fees and staking. Denominated in MIST (1 SUI = 1,000,000,000 MIST).
The smallest unit of SUI. 1 SUI = 1,000,000,000 MIST.
The smart contract programming language used on Sui, derived from the Move language originally created for Diem. Sui Move features asset-oriented types and object-centric storage.
A collection of Move modules published on-chain as a single immutable object. Identified by its ObjectID.
A compilable unit within a Move package containing type definitions and function implementations.
The price per unit of gas, expressed in MIST. Used to calculate transaction fees. A reference gas price is published by the network each epoch.
The maximum amount of gas (in MIST) the sender is willing to pay for a transaction.
A stake delegation to a validator, represented as a StakedSui object. Earns staking rewards each epoch.
A node operator that participates in Sui consensus and produces checkpoints. Validators are elected each epoch based on delegated stake.
A 32-byte address on the Sui network, represented as a 0x-prefixed 64-character hex string. Derived from a public key.
A key-value pair attached to a parent Sui object, allowing objects to hold heterogeneous collections of child objects.
A compound identifier for an event consisting of a transaction digest and event sequence number.
An event emitted by a Move function during transaction execution. Events are indexed and queryable.
The Sui Name Service (SuiNS) provides human-readable names (e.g., example.sui) that resolve to Sui addresses.
A flexible transaction format that allows composing multiple Move calls, coin operations, and object transfers in a single atomic transaction.
A Sui authentication primitive that allows users to sign transactions using OAuth credentials (Google, Facebook, etc.) via zero-knowledge proofs.
Binary Canonical Serialization. The serialization format used for Sui transactions and objects. Used in API parameters expecting encoded data.
Google Remote Procedure Call. The recommended API protocol for Sui full nodes as of the v2 API, replacing JSON-RPC. Uses Protocol Buffers.
A query language for APIs. Sui provides a GraphQL RPC endpoint backed by the General-Purpose Indexer for flexible data queries.
A remote procedure call protocol encoded in JSON. The legacy Sui API protocol, deprecated as of 2025, to be decommissioned July 31, 2026.
